project.name: Marketing OS — Telegram Bot
project.description: AI-powered marketing strategy platform for Vietnamese founders & business owners, combining a Telegram bot with multi-agent pipelines (market research, competitor analysis, customer insight, pricing psychology, social listening, strategy) and a web dashboard.

nodes.name: fb_ads_library.py
nodes.summary: Wraps the Facebook Ads Library API to search competitor ads (by keyword or page id), resolve Facebook page URLs to page ids, and format ad results for downstream LLM analysis.

nodes.name: search_competitor_ads
nodes.summary: Searches the Facebook Ads Library for competitor ads matching a search term and country/region filters.

nodes.name: search_by_page_id
nodes.summary: Searches the Facebook Ads Library for all active ads run by a specific page id.

nodes.name: format_ads_for_analysis
nodes.summary: Formats raw Ads Library API results into a structured text block suitable for LLM-based competitor analysis.

nodes.name: is_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether the Facebook Ads Library integration is configured and usable.

nodes.name: _extract_fb_candidate
nodes.summary: Extracts a candidate Facebook page identifier or URL fragment from arbitrary user-provided text.

nodes.name: resolve_fb_url
nodes.summary: Resolves a Facebook URL or vanity name to a canonical page id by querying the Graph API or Ads Library.

nodes.name: resolve_page_id_from_url
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that extracts a Facebook candidate from text and resolves it to a page id.

nodes.name: fb_marketing.py
nodes.summary: Wraps the Facebook Marketing API to fetch account/ad-level insights, manage campaigns and ad sets (status changes, budget updates), and format performance data for LLM-based analysis and the ads optimizer.

nodes.name: get_account_insights
nodes.summary: Fetches aggregate ad account performance insights (spend, ROAS, CPA, etc.) from the Facebook Marketing API over a date range.

nodes.name: get_account_insights_daily
nodes.summary: Fetches day-by-day breakdown of ad account performance insights from the Facebook Marketing API.

nodes.name: get_active_campaigns
nodes.summary: Fetches the list of currently active campaigns for an ad account from the Facebook Marketing API.

nodes.name: _extract_action_value
nodes.summary: Extracts a specific action's metric value (e.g. purchases) from a Facebook Insights actions array.

nodes.name: _hook_hold_rates
nodes.summary: Computes video hook and hold retention rates from Facebook video metrics.

nodes.name: format_insights_for_analysis
nodes.summary: Formats account-level insights data into a structured text block for LLM-based performance analysis.

nodes.name: format_ad_level_for_analysis
nodes.summary: Formats ad-level performance data (including hook/hold rates) into a structured text block for LLM-based ad analysis.

nodes.name: is_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether the Facebook Marketing API integration is configured and usable.

nodes.name: has_token
nodes.summary: Checks whether a valid Facebook access token is available for API calls.

nodes.name: get_campaigns_with_adsets
nodes.summary: Fetches campaigns along with their nested ad sets from the Facebook Marketing API for the ads optimizer hierarchy view.

nodes.name: get_ads_by_adset
nodes.summary: Fetches all ads belonging to a specific ad set from the Facebook Marketing API.

nodes.name: set_object_status
nodes.summary: Updates the active/paused status of a Facebook campaign, ad set, or ad object via the Marketing API.

nodes.name: update_budget
nodes.summary: Updates the daily or lifetime budget of a Facebook campaign or ad set via the Marketing API.

nodes.name: format_hierarchy_for_optimizer
nodes.summary: Formats the campaign/ad-set/ad hierarchy with performance metrics into a structured text block for the LLM-driven ads optimizer.

nodes.name: image_gen.py
nodes.summary: Wraps an AI image generation/editing provider to generate marketing images, edit existing images, analyze image style, and estimate generation cost.

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the client used for image generation API calls.

nodes.name: generate_image
nodes.summary: Generates a new marketing image from a text prompt using the configured AI image generation provider.

nodes.name: edit_image
nodes.summary: Edits an existing image according to a text instruction using the AI image editing provider.

nodes.name: analyze_image_style
nodes.summary: Analyzes an uploaded image's visual style attributes via the AI vision provider for use in style-matched generation.

nodes.name: estimate_cost
nodes.summary: Estimates the monetary cost of an image generation or editing request based on provider pricing.

nodes.name: is_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether the image generation integration is configured and usable.

nodes.name: llm_router.py
nodes.summary: Multi-provider LLM router abstracting Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, and Perplexity behind a unified call() interface with task-type-based provider selection, fallback chains, and availability reporting.

nodes.name: Provider
nodes.summary: Enum of supported LLM providers (Anthropic, Gemini, OpenAI, Perplexity) used to select which backend handles a given call.

nodes.name: TaskType
nodes.summary: Enum of task categories (e.g. reasoning, fast-extraction, grounded-search) used to map requests to the most appropriate provider/model.

nodes.name: ProviderUnavailable
nodes.summary: Exception raised when a specific LLM provider is not configured or unreachable for a requested call.

nodes.name: AllProvidersFailedError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when every provider in the fallback chain fails to fulfill a call.

nodes.name: _get_anthropic_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the Anthropic client instance.

nodes.name: _call_anthropic_sonnet
nodes.summary: Invokes the Anthropic Claude Sonnet model with the given prompt and parameters.

nodes.name: _call_anthropic_haiku
nodes.summary: Invokes the Anthropic Claude Haiku model with the given prompt and parameters, used for fast/cheap extraction tasks.

nodes.name: _get_gemini_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the Google Gemini client instance.

nodes.name: _calc_thinking_budget
nodes.summary: Computes the reasoning/thinking token budget to allocate for a Gemini call based on task complexity.

nodes.name: _call_gemini_pro
nodes.summary: Invokes the Gemini Pro model with the given prompt, handling thinking budget and response parsing.

nodes.name: _patch_homepage_links
nodes.summary: Post-processes Gemini grounded-search responses to fix or inject homepage links into the generated content.

nodes.name: _call_gemini_pro_grounded
nodes.summary: Invokes the Gemini Pro model with web-grounded search enabled for tasks requiring up-to-date factual grounding.

nodes.name: _call_gemini_flash
nodes.summary: Invokes the faster, cheaper Gemini Flash model for lighter-weight tasks.

nodes.name: _get_openai_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the OpenAI client instance.

nodes.name: _call_openai_generic
nodes.summary: Generic helper that invokes a specified OpenAI model with the given prompt and parameters, shared by the model-specific wrapper functions.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt5
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-5 model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt5_mini
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-5 Mini model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt5_nano
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-5 Nano model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt_4_1_mini
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-4.1 Mini model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt4o
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-4o model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_openai_gpt4o_mini
nodes.summary: Invokes the OpenAI GPT-4o Mini model via the generic OpenAI call helper.

nodes.name: _call_perplexity_sonar
nodes.summary: Invokes the Perplexity Sonar model, typically used for web-grounded research queries.

nodes.name: call
nodes.summary: Main entrypoint of the LLM router: selects a provider chain based on TaskType, attempts each provider in order with fallback, and raises AllProvidersFailedError if all fail.

nodes.name: _forced_test_provider
nodes.summary: Allows tests/debugging to force the router to use a specific provider, bypassing normal selection logic.

nodes.name: is_provider_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether a given provider's API key/credentials are configured and usable.

nodes.name: availability_report
nodes.summary: Builds a summary report of which LLM providers are currently available/configured.

nodes.name: video_vision.py
nodes.summary: Extracts keyframes from uploaded videos via ffmpeg probing/extraction and analyzes them with an AI vision model to produce a visual analysis summary for marketing content review.

nodes.name: is_available
nodes.summary: Checks whether the video vision analysis integration is configured and usable.

nodes.name: availability_report
nodes.summary: Builds a summary report describing the availability status of the video vision integration.

nodes.name: extract_keyframes
nodes.summary: Extracts a set of representative keyframes from a video file at selected timestamps using ffmpeg.

nodes.name: _choose_timestamps
nodes.summary: Chooses a set of evenly-spaced or heuristically-significant timestamps within a video's duration for keyframe extraction.

nodes.name: _probe_duration
nodes.summary: Probes a video file's total duration using ffprobe.

nodes.name: _extract_one_frame
nodes.summary: Extracts a single frame from a video at a specified timestamp using ffmpeg.

nodes.name: analyze_frames_with_vision
nodes.summary: Sends extracted video keyframes to an AI vision model for analysis (e.g. content quality, hook strength).

nodes.name: extract_visual_analysis
nodes.summary: High-level orchestration function that extracts keyframes from a video and runs vision analysis on them, returning a combined visual analysis result.

nodes.name: feedback_digest.py
nodes.summary: Background worker that formats and runs a periodic digest job summarizing recent user feedback for review.

nodes.name: format_digest
nodes.summary: Formats aggregated feedback digest data into a readable summary report.

nodes.name: run_once
nodes.summary: Runs a single pass of the feedback digest worker: gathers digest data, formats it, and delivers/logs the result.

nodes.name: monitor_competitors.py
nodes.summary: Background worker that periodically checks tracked competitors' Facebook ad activity via the Ads Library and notifies the bot/user of new ads detected.

nodes.name: _ad_ids_from_response
nodes.summary: Extracts the set of ad ids present in a Facebook Ads Library API response.

nodes.name: check_one
nodes.summary: Checks a single tracked competitor for new ad activity since the last check, comparing current ad ids to the previously stored set.

nodes.name: run_once_with_bot
nodes.summary: Runs a single monitoring pass across all due tracked competitors and sends Telegram notifications via the bot for any new ads found.

nodes.name: start_background_monitor
nodes.summary: Starts a recurring background task that periodically triggers the competitor monitoring check.

nodes.name: run_once
nodes.summary: Runs a single monitoring pass across all due tracked competitors without requiring a bot instance, primarily for manual/CLI invocation.

nodes.name: brand_voice.py
nodes.summary: Storage layer for brand voice profiles: reads/writes versioned brand voice records (tone, rules, banned words) to the database and supports deactivation.

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the database client used for brand voice queries.

nodes.name: _row_to_bv
nodes.summary: Converts a raw database row into a BrandVoice model instance, coercing/defaulting fields as needed.

nodes.name: get_brand_voice
nodes.summary: Fetches the most recently updated active brand voice record for a user from the database.

nodes.name: save_brand_voice
nodes.summary: Deactivates the user's previous active brand voice, computes the next version number, and inserts a new brand voice record.

nodes.name: has_brand_voice
nodes.summary: Checks whether a user has a non-empty active brand voice profile saved.

nodes.name: deactivate_brand_voice
nodes.summary: Marks all of a user's brand voice records as inactive in the database.

nodes.name: campaign_history.py
nodes.summary: Storage layer for past campaign runs: builds and saves campaign summaries with embeddings, and supports semantic similarity search plus listing past campaigns.

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the database client used for campaign history queries.

nodes.name: _truncate
nodes.summary: Truncates a text string to a maximum character length.

nodes.name: _build_summary
nodes.summary: Builds a condensed natural-language summary of a session's pipeline results for storage and future embedding-based retrieval.

nodes.name: save_campaign_history
nodes.summary: Builds a campaign summary, generates its embedding, and inserts a new campaign history record into the database.

nodes.name: search_similar_campaigns
nodes.summary: Embeds a query string and performs a vector similarity RPC search against stored campaign histories, falling back to a recency-ordered listing on failure.

nodes.name: list_campaigns
nodes.summary: Lists a user's past campaigns ordered by creation date, limited to a max count.

nodes.name: embeddings.py
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around the OpenAI embeddings API to convert text into vector embeddings for semantic search.

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches the OpenAI client used to generate embeddings.

nodes.name: embed_text
nodes.summary: Generates a vector embedding for a given text string via the OpenAI embeddings API.

nodes.name: feedback_log.py
nodes.summary: Storage layer for user feedback: logs feedback events, retrieves recent feedback, and builds digest summaries for periodic reporting.

nodes.name: log_feedback
nodes.summary: Inserts a new feedback log entry for a user into the database.

nodes.name: get_recent_feedback
nodes.summary: Retrieves the most recent feedback log entries for a user.

nodes.name: get_digest_summary
nodes.summary: Aggregates recent feedback entries into a summary structure used to build periodic feedback digests.

nodes.name: session.py
nodes.summary: Core session persistence layer: manages the DB connection pool, normalizes/migrates v1-to-v2 session row formats, and provides get/save/reset operations for user sessions.

nodes.name: init_pool
nodes.summary: Initializes the shared async database connection pool used by session storage.

nodes.name: init_db
nodes.summary: Ensures the sessions database schema/tables exist, creating them if necessary.

nodes.name: _normalize_results
nodes.summary: Normalizes a session's stored pipeline results dict into a consistent versioned structure.

nodes.name: _row_to_session
nodes.summary: Converts a raw database row into a Session model instance, handling v1-to-v2 migration of legacy fields.

nodes.name: get_session
nodes.summary: Fetches a user's session row from the database and converts it into a Session model, creating a default if none exists.

nodes.name: save_session
nodes.summary: Persists a Session model's current state back to the database, upserting the row and serializing nested results.

nodes.name: _clear_v1_row
nodes.summary: Clears legacy v1-format fields from a session row during migration to v2.

nodes.name: reset_session
nodes.summary: Resets a user's session to a fresh default state in the database, clearing prior pipeline results and profile data.

nodes.name: tracked_competitors.py
nodes.summary: Storage layer for competitor tracking: add/remove tracked competitors, list by user, and find competitors due for a periodic ad-monitoring check.

nodes.name: add_tracked
nodes.summary: Adds a new competitor (by Facebook page) to a user's tracked competitor list in the database.

nodes.name: remove_tracked
nodes.summary: Removes a tracked competitor entry for a user from the database.

nodes.name: list_tracked_by_user
nodes.summary: Lists all competitors currently tracked by a given user.

nodes.name: get_due_tracked
nodes.summary: Finds tracked competitors whose next scheduled monitoring check is due.

nodes.name: update_after_check
nodes.summary: Updates a tracked competitor's last-checked timestamp and state after a monitoring run completes.

nodes.name: crypto.py
nodes.summary: Provides Fernet-based symmetric encryption/decryption helpers for securely storing sensitive tokens (e.g. Facebook OAuth tokens) at rest.

nodes.name: _get_fernet
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches a Fernet cipher instance from the configured encryption key.

nodes.name: encrypt_token
nodes.summary: Encrypts a plaintext token string using Fernet symmetric encryption.

nodes.name: decrypt_token
nodes.summary: Decrypts a previously Fernet-encrypted token string back to plaintext.

nodes.name: max_conductor.py
nodes.summary: Routes free-form user messages to an intent + parameters using an Anthropic LLM call, used to dispatch follow-up requests in the Telegram bot (e.g. 'change channel to Instagram').

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches a module-level AsyncAnthropic client instance.

nodes.name: route_intent
nodes.summary: Sends the user's free-text message and session context to Claude, parses the JSON response to determine intent and parameters for routing the conversation.

nodes.name: post_actions.py
nodes.summary: Parses generated content calendars into structured posts and provides LLM-backed actions (edit, adapt to channel, generate variant) plus preview formatting for Telegram.

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches a module-level AsyncAnthropic client instance.

nodes.name: parse_calendar_to_posts
nodes.summary: Splits raw calendar text into individual post blocks and tags each with detected channel, week/day, and content pillar.

nodes.name: _detect_channel
nodes.summary: Heuristically detects the marketing channel (e.g. Facebook, Instagram) referenced in a block of text.

nodes.name: _detect_week_day
nodes.summary: Extracts a week number and day-of-week label from a calendar text block using regex and keyword matching.

nodes.name: _detect_pillar
nodes.summary: Classifies a content block into a marketing pillar (educate/trust/engage/convert) based on keyword matches.

nodes.name: edit_post
nodes.summary: Calls the LLM to revise an existing post's content according to a free-text instruction.

nodes.name: adapt_post
nodes.summary: Calls the LLM to rewrite a post's content for a different target channel using the session's business profile context.

nodes.name: gen_variant
nodes.summary: Generates an alternative variant of a post's content via the LLM using the session's business profile context.

nodes.name: format_post_preview
nodes.summary: Formats a post dict into a human-readable preview string (with metadata) for display in Telegram, truncated to a max character count.

nodes.name: tone_calibration.py
nodes.summary: Implements the brand-voice tone calibration flow: parses a sample post from a calendar, generates sample posts, extracts tone signals from user feedback, and regenerates content/calendars to match the calibrated tone.

nodes.name: _get_client
nodes.summary: Lazily constructs and caches a module-level AsyncAnthropic client instance.

nodes.name: parse_first_post
nodes.summary: Parses the first markdown-table-formatted post row out of a generated content calendar using regex table detection and flexible column header matching.

nodes.name: generate_sample_post
nodes.summary: Generates a sample post via the LLM for a given calendar row using the brand voice profile and copywriting framework guidance, for the user to give tone feedback on.

nodes.name: extract_tone_signals
nodes.summary: Uses the LLM to extract structured tone signals (e.g. preferred style, banned words) from the user's free-text feedback on a sample post.

nodes.name: regen_post_with_signals
nodes.summary: Regenerates a post's content via the LLM, applying the extracted tone signals as additional style guidance.

nodes.name: apply_tone_to_calendar
nodes.summary: Applies the calibrated tone signals across an entire generated content calendar via the LLM, using the approved sample post as a style reference.

nodes.name: format_signals_card
nodes.summary: Formats extracted tone signals into a readable summary card for display to the user in Telegram.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Central configuration module loading environment variables and constants (API keys, tokens, feature flags) used across the entire application.

nodes.name: run_local.py
nodes.summary: Local entrypoint for the Telegram bot: initializes storage pools/DB, registers all command/message/callback handlers on a python-telegram-bot Application, and runs polling.

nodes.name: post_init
nodes.summary: Post-initialization hook run after the Telegram Application is built; initializes the DB connection pool and database schema.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Builds the Telegram Application, registers all command/message/callback handlers, and starts polling for updates.

nodes.name: fb_oauth.py
nodes.summary: Implements the Facebook OAuth flow: builds the authorization URL, exchanges/extends access tokens, fetches ad accounts, handles the OAuth callback, and notifies the user in Telegram once connected.

nodes.name: build_oauth_url
nodes.summary: Builds the Facebook OAuth authorization URL for a given user, persisting a random state token for CSRF protection.

nodes.name: _exchange_short_token
nodes.summary: Exchanges the OAuth authorization code for a short-lived Facebook access token via the Graph API.

nodes.name: _extend_to_long_token
nodes.summary: Exchanges a short-lived Facebook access token for a long-lived one and computes its expiry timestamp.

nodes.name: _get_ad_accounts
nodes.summary: Fetches the list of Facebook ad accounts accessible to the authenticated user via the Graph API.

nodes.name: refresh_token_if_needed
nodes.summary: Checks whether a user's stored Facebook token is near expiry and refreshes/extends it if needed, disabling the connection on failure.

nodes.name: _norm_id
nodes.summary: Normalizes a Facebook ad account id, stripping the 'act_' prefix for consistent comparisons.

nodes.name: handle_callback
nodes.summary: FastAPI-style callback handler for the Facebook OAuth redirect: validates state, exchanges tokens, fetches ad accounts, saves the connection, and either auto-selects or prompts the user to choose an account.

nodes.name: _ask_account_selection
nodes.summary: Sends a Telegram message with inline buttons letting the user pick which Facebook ad account to connect when multiple are available.

nodes.name: _notify_connected
nodes.summary: Sends a Telegram confirmation message to the user once a Facebook ad account has been successfully connected.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init for the storage module; re-exports brand voice, campaign history, embeddings, models, and session storage submodules for convenient imports.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the agents module that re-exports the pipeline orchestrator as the public entry point.

nodes.name: agent_wrappers.py
nodes.summary: Thin async wrapper functions around the strategic AI skills (market research, competitor analysis, pricing, SWOT, retention/winback, synthesis) that adapt provider calls into the pipeline's agent interface.

nodes.name: content_suite_prompts.py
nodes.summary: Static prompt template constants for the content generation suite (posts, captions, video scripts) consumed by operational skills.

nodes.name: critic.py
nodes.summary: Runs an LLM-based quality critic pass over agent output, detecting meta-review artifacts and injecting hyperlinks to known sources before returning a reviewed result.

nodes.name: operational_prompts.py
nodes.summary: Large library of static prompt template constants for operational marketing skills (ads, sales scripts, email/Zalo sequences, UGC briefs).

nodes.name: operational_skill.py
nodes.summary: Defines the base OperationalSkillConfig and OperationalSkill classes that establish the contract (context building, user message building, intake field tracking) for all operational marketing skills.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a dataclass-style config object paired with a base class implementing template-method style hooks overridden by concrete skill subclasses defined elsewhere.

nodes.name: OperationalSkillConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration data object describing metadata (name, intake fields, output format) for a single operational skill.

nodes.name: OperationalSkill
nodes.summary: Base class for operational marketing skills providing context/user-message construction and intake-field validation that concrete skill subclasses extend.

nodes.name: operational_skills_config.py
nodes.summary: Central registry and factory module defining all concrete operational skill classes (campaign briefs, content calendars, ads copy, video scripts, competitor comparison, brand positioning) and a lookup function to instantiate them by task name.
nodes.languageNotes: Large module aggregating ~10 skill subclasses and ~24 factory/helper functions; a strong candidate for splitting by skill domain in future refactors.

nodes.name: ContentGeneratorPipeline
nodes.summary: Pipeline-style skill that pre-fills intake data and runs the multi-step content generation workflow.

nodes.name: AdsIntelligencePipeline
nodes.summary: Pipeline-style skill that pre-fills intake and executes the ads intelligence/competitor analysis workflow.

nodes.name: CompetitorComparisonSkill
nodes.summary: Operational skill that builds context and prompts for comparing the business against named competitors.

nodes.name: BrandPositioningSkill
nodes.summary: Operational skill that builds context and prompts for generating a brand positioning statement.

nodes.name: AdsOptimizerSkill
nodes.summary: Operational skill that builds context and prompts for ad-spend optimization recommendations.

nodes.name: CampaignBriefDynamicSkill
nodes.summary: Operational skill that dynamically builds a campaign brief prompt based on collected intake answers.

nodes.name: ContentCalendarDynamicSkill
nodes.summary: Operational skill that dynamically generates a content calendar prompt, factoring in pillar mix and cadence.

nodes.name: AdsCopySkill
nodes.summary: Operational skill that builds context and prompts for generating ad copy variants.

nodes.name: VideoScriptsSkill
nodes.summary: Operational skill that builds context and prompts for generating short-form video scripts.

nodes.name: ViralVideoAnalyzerSkill
nodes.summary: Operational skill that analyzes viral video transcripts/visual analysis and resolves them into structured insights for the prompt context.

nodes.name: _config_for
nodes.summary: Builds an OperationalSkillConfig object for a given task name from shared defaults.

nodes.name: calc_dynamic_pillar_mix
nodes.summary: Computes the content-pillar distribution mix for a content calendar based on business profile and goals.

nodes.name: _run_async_sync
nodes.summary: Helper that runs an async coroutine to completion from synchronous code, bridging sync/async boundaries.

nodes.name: get_operational_skill
nodes.summary: Factory/registry lookup that returns the appropriate OperationalSkill instance for a given task type.

nodes.name: output_formats.py
nodes.summary: Defines output-format and language-instruction string templates used to steer LLM response structure (e.g. bullet lists, tables) and localization level.

nodes.name: get_format_instruction
nodes.summary: Returns the prompt instruction text corresponding to a requested output format.

nodes.name: pipeline.py
nodes.summary: Core orchestration module that sequences the multi-agent marketing strategy pipeline — intake, market research, competitor analysis, pricing, SWOT, retention/winback, synthesis, and operational skill execution — and persists results to the session/history store.
nodes.languageNotes: Acts as the central coordinator that wires together skills, critics, and storage; one of the largest and most structurally significant files in the project.

nodes.name: StageConfig
nodes.summary: Small config object describing a pipeline stage's name, runner function, and metadata used by the targeted/multi-agent pipeline runners.

nodes.name: run_intake
nodes.summary: Runs the intake conversation flow, extracting a structured business profile from the user's free-text answers.

nodes.name: _extract_profile_from_response
nodes.summary: Parses the LLM intake response text into a structured BusinessProfile object.

nodes.name: _run_agent
nodes.summary: Generic helper that invokes a strategic agent wrapper function and records its output to the session.

nodes.name: _run_skill
nodes.summary: Generic operational-skill execution helper that builds skill context/prompts, calls the LLM provider, runs critic review, and stores the versioned result.

nodes.name: _competitor_grounded_pass
nodes.summary: Runs a grounded follow-up pass over the competitor analysis stage to enrich it with verified data.

nodes.name: run_retention_strategy_stage
nodes.summary: Runs the retention strategy stage of the strategic pipeline and records the result.

nodes.name: run_winback_vision_stage
nodes.summary: Runs the win-back vision stage of the strategic pipeline and records the result.

nodes.name: run_usp_definition
nodes.summary: Runs the USP (unique selling proposition) definition stage of the strategic pipeline.

nodes.name: run_operational_skill
nodes.summary: Entry point that looks up and executes a named operational skill against the current session via _run_skill.

nodes.name: run_strategic_single_skill
nodes.summary: Runs a single strategic stage in isolation, used when the user wants to redo or fetch one piece of strategy.

nodes.name: run_full_pipeline
nodes.summary: Runs the complete end-to-end strategic pipeline across all stages in sequence for a session.

nodes.name: run_targeted_pipeline
nodes.summary: Runs a subset of pipeline stages targeted at specific user-selected outputs rather than the full sequence.

nodes.name: run_multi_agent_targeted
nodes.summary: Coordinates execution of multiple targeted agent stages concurrently/sequentially based on a stage configuration list.

nodes.name: prompts.py
nodes.summary: Large library of static system/user prompt template constants for the strategic pipeline stages (intake, market research, competitor, customer, pricing, social).

nodes.name: get_intake_system
nodes.summary: Returns the appropriate intake system prompt template based on the requested task type.

nodes.name: skills.py
nodes.summary: Defines the AgentSkill base class and concrete strategic skill classes (market research, competitor, customer insight, pricing psychology, social listening, USP, SWOT, strategy synthesis, tactical playbook) that build LLM context and prompts for each strategic stage.

nodes.name: AgentSkill
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for strategic agent skills defining the build_context/build_user_msg contract.

nodes.name: MarketResearchSkill
nodes.summary: Strategic skill that builds prompts for the market research stage of the pipeline.

nodes.name: CompetitorSkill
nodes.summary: Strategic skill that builds prompts for the competitor analysis stage of the pipeline.

nodes.name: CustomerInsightSkill
nodes.summary: Strategic skill that builds prompts for the customer insight analysis stage of the pipeline.

nodes.name: PsychologyPricingSkill
nodes.summary: Strategic skill that builds prompts for the psychology-based pricing recommendation stage.

nodes.name: SocialListeningSkill
nodes.summary: Strategic skill that builds prompts for the social listening analysis stage.

nodes.name: UspDefinitionSkill
nodes.summary: Strategic skill that builds prompts for defining the business's unique selling proposition.

nodes.name: StrategySynthesisSkill
nodes.summary: Strategic skill that synthesizes all prior pipeline stage outputs into a cohesive strategy summary.

nodes.name: SwotSkill

nodes.name: sessions
nodes.summary: Initial migration creating the sessions table that tracks each user's onboarding stage, intake history, profile, and accumulated results, with supporting indexes on stage and update time.

nodes.name: tracked_competitors
nodes.summary: Migration creating the tracked_competitors table used to monitor competitor ad activity per user, storing the last seen ad IDs and indexed for due-for-check polling.

nodes.name: feedback_log
nodes.summary: Migration creating the feedback_log table that records user ratings and feedback text on generated skill outputs, indexed by skill/industry and by low ratings for quality review.

nodes.name: user_brand_voice
nodes.summary: Migration creating the user_brand_voice table that versions and stores each user's brand voice profile, with an active-version flag and an index for quick lookup by user.

nodes.name: user_campaign_history
nodes.summary: Migration enabling the pgvector extension and creating the user_campaign_history table, which stores past campaign briefs and an embedding column for similarity search across industry, stage, and competitor context.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the pgvector extension to add a vector embedding column with a dedicated similarity index, enabling semantic retrieval of past campaign history.

nodes.name: users
nodes.summary: Large normalization migration that restructures the schema into six core tables (users, user_business_profile, user_sessions_slim, skill_runs, campaigns, posts) replacing earlier denormalized session data, with extensive indexing for active users, quotas, and skill/campaign lookups.
nodes.languageNotes: A large-scale normalization migration splitting a monolithic sessions table into six purpose-specific tables with foreign-key-style relations and 14 supporting indexes.

nodes.name: user_business_profile
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; stores each user's business profile attributes (industry, stage, target customer, revenue, budget, channels, competitors) split out from the original sessions table.

nodes.name: user_sessions_slim
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; a lean per-user session table tracking the currently selected task, intake history, and tone calibration, replacing the bulkier original sessions table.

nodes.name: skill_runs
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; logs each invocation of a marketing skill per user, including generated content, model used, and rating, indexed for recent and per-skill lookups.

nodes.name: campaigns
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; represents a marketing campaign per user with an offer lever, end date, embedding, and link back to the originating skill run, indexed for active-campaign queries.

nodes.name: posts
nodes.summary: Part of the normalization migration; tracks individual scheduled content posts belonging to a campaign, with day, status, and link fields, indexed by user/channel and parent campaign.

nodes.name: engagements
nodes.summary: Migration introducing the engagement-centric data spine: creates engagements, diagnostic_briefs, and strategies tables to model a structured consulting workflow per user, replacing ad-hoc campaign tracking.
nodes.languageNotes: Introduces a hierarchical engagement -> diagnostic brief -> strategy data model with versioning and token-usage tracking, supported by 10 indexes.

nodes.name: diagnostic_briefs
nodes.summary: Part of the engagement spine migration; stores diagnostic brief documents generated for an engagement, tracking token usage and creation time.

nodes.name: strategies
nodes.summary: Part of the engagement spine migration; stores versioned marketing strategy documents tied to an engagement and diagnostic brief, including content pillars, KPI dashboard, model used, and user feedback rating.

nodes.name: user_sessions_slim (v2 fields)
nodes.summary: Small migration adding missing V2 columns (pending_followup_skill, content_outputs, token_log) to user_sessions_slim and a cost_used_usd column to users, fixing gaps from the V1-to-V2 session adapter cutover.

nodes.name: campaign_intelligence
nodes.summary: Migration creating the campaign_intelligence table that captures industry-specific campaign intelligence events per user, indexed by industry, event, and creation time.

nodes.name: user_fb_connections
nodes.summary: Migration creating Facebook integration tables: user_fb_connections for OAuth-linked ad accounts, oauth_states for the OAuth handshake, ads_snapshots for daily ad performance metrics, and ads_alert_cooldowns to throttle repeat alerts.

nodes.name: oauth_states
nodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; stores short-lived OAuth state tokens used to validate the Facebook OAuth callback flow per user.

nodes.name: ads_snapshots
nodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; stores daily snapshots of Facebook ad campaign performance metrics (impressions, reach, clicks, CTR, CPM, leads, purchases) per user, indexed by user and date.

nodes.name: ads_alert_cooldowns
nodes.summary: Part of the Facebook connections migration; tracks cooldown state per user/campaign/alert-type to prevent duplicate ad performance alerts from being sent repeatedly.

nodes.name: user_fb_connections (available_accounts)
nodes.summary: Tiny migration adding an available_accounts JSONB column to user_fb_connections so users can switch between Facebook ad accounts without re-authenticating via OAuth.

nodes.name: code-reviewer.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code subagent definition for a senior code-reviewer persona that evaluates changes across correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance, producing a categorized review output.

nodes.name: security-auditor.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code subagent definition for a security-auditor persona that reviews input handling, auth, data protection, infrastructure, and AI/LLM features, classifying findings by severity.

nodes.name: test-engineer.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code subagent definition for a QA/test-engineer persona that designs test suites, writes tests, and analyzes coverage gaps using a structured analyze-then-write approach.

nodes.name: web-performance-auditor.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code subagent definition for a web-performance-auditor persona covering Core Web Vitals, loading, rendering, and network optimization, with quick and deep (tool-assisted) operating modes.

nodes.name: build.md
nodes.summary: Defines the `/build` slash command, which implements plan tasks incrementally via a RED-GREEN-verify-commit loop, with an `auto` mode that runs the entire approved plan without stopping between tasks.
nodes.languageNotes: Claude Code slash-command markdown with YAML frontmatter description and `$ARGUMENTS` mode dispatch.

nodes.name: code-simplify.md
nodes.summary: Defines the `/code-simplify` slash command, which guides simplification of recently changed code while preserving behavior, covering nesting, function length, naming, duplication, and dead code.

nodes.name: plan.md
nodes.summary: Defines the `/plan` slash command, which breaks work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering, saving output to tasks/plan.md and tasks/todo.md.

nodes.name: review.md
nodes.summary: Defines the `/review` slash command, which conducts a five-axis code review covering correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance with categorized findings.

nodes.name: ship.md
nodes.summary: Defines the `/ship` slash command, a fan-out orchestrator that runs code-reviewer, security-auditor, and test-engineer subagents in parallel, then merges their reports into a single go/no-go launch decision with a mandatory rollback plan.
nodes.languageNotes: Documents a parallel multi-agent fan-out/merge pattern using Claude Code's Agent tool and subagent_type dispatch.

nodes.name: spec.md
nodes.summary: Defines the `/spec` slash command, which starts spec-driven development by gathering requirements and generating a structured SPEC.md before any code is written.

nodes.name: test.md
nodes.summary: Defines the `/test` slash command, which runs the test-driven development workflow for new features and the Prove-It pattern for bug fixes, including a hook to browser-based verification for UI issues.

nodes.name: webperf.md
nodes.summary: Defines the `/webperf` slash command, which runs a web performance audit via the web-performance-auditor subagent, supporting a Deep mode using Lighthouse/PSI/CrUX/DevTools data and a Quick mode based on static source scanning.

nodes.name: SKILL.md
nodes.summary: Defines the 'idea-refine' Claude Code skill: a three-phase (Understand & Expand, Evaluate & Converge, Sharpen & Ship) ideation partner that turns a vague idea into a concrete markdown one-pager with problem statement, recommended direction, assumptions, MVP scope, and a 'not doing' list.
nodes.languageNotes: Claude Code skill markdown with YAML frontmatter (name/description) followed by structured trigger phrases, process phases, anti-patterns, and a verification checklist.

nodes.name: examples.md
nodes.summary: Worked examples illustrating the idea-refine skill's three-phase process across a vague early-stage concept, a feature idea within an existing codebase, and a non-product workflow idea, ending with takeaways on what makes a good ideation session.

nodes.name: frameworks.md
nodes.summary: Reference catalog of ideation frameworks (SCAMPER, How Might We, First Principles, Jobs to Be Done, Constraint-Based Ideation, Pre-mortem, Analogous Inspiration) used to generate idea variations during Phase 1 of the idea-refine skill.

nodes.name: refinement-criteria.md
nodes.summary: Evaluation rubric for stress-testing idea directions during Phase 2 of the idea-refine skill, covering user value, feasibility, and differentiation dimensions, an assumption audit (must/should/might be true), and MVP scoping principles.

nodes.name: .env.example

nodes.name: railway.json

nodes.name: requirements.txt

nodes.name: ADS_SCHEDULER_SETUP.md
nodes.summary: Setup guide for the Ads Scheduler background job, covering database migration, encryption key generation, Facebook App OAuth configuration, Railway environment variables, deployment verification, and the available alert-threshold commands and benchmarks.

nodes.name: APP_FUNCTIONS.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive functional reference for the Marketing OS bot describing overall architecture, Telegram commands, the Intake/Strategic/Operational/Analysis skill layers, pipelines and orchestrators, the Ads Scheduler reporting job, core background systems (LLM router, Industry Brain, Brand Voice, tone calibration, campaign history), and a migration checklist of what to carry into a new repo.

nodes.name: BACKLOG.md
nodes.summary: Running backlog log of completed product/engineering fixes and improvements (dated entries) covering competitor comparison, dead-code removal, ads generator gaps, content calendar restructuring, strategic-question flow dependencies, and budget/team-context sequencing changes.

nodes.name: DB_V2_MIGRATION_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Step-by-step runbook for migrating to a v2 database schema via dual-write, covering Supabase migration application, backfill scripts, Railway branch switching, drift detection, cutover to v2 reads, post-cutover cleanup, and an emergency rollback matrix with success KPIs.

nodes.name: IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md
nodes.summary: Workflow 2.0 implementation plan with locked architecture decisions (Strategic, Campaign Bridge, Brand Voice, Content Execution layers), a sprint-by-sprint breakdown (DB schema, USP layer, retention integration, campaign brief, brand voice persistence, tone calibration, multi-agent orchestrator), dependency/risk registers, and a final roadmap.

nodes.name: LAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md
nodes.summary: Short pre-launch checklist enumerating security gate items that must be resolved before opening the product to paying customers.

nodes.name: MAX_CMO_REFERENCE.md

nodes.name: SCALE_BACKLOG.md
nodes.summary: Backlog of future-facing items to tackle when scaling the product or preparing it for sale to C-level/agency customers, including economics/feasibility gating, closing the measurement loop, and a multi-skin Strategy Object (Owner/C-level/Agency) concept.

nodes.name: markos-flow.md
nodes.summary: End-to-end walkthrough of the MarkOS user journey across all layers: entry/onboarding, profile-check gate, multi-turn intake, the six-stage strategic pipeline, operational skills (content calendar, content generator pipeline), tone calibration, LLM routing, Excel rendering paths, rating/feedback loop, and the full state-machine flag inventory, plus a list of known gaps from a prior audit.

nodes.name: ARCHITECTURE.md
nodes.summary: Current-state architecture map for the markOS Web App: SPA frontend talking to a Starlette backend (webapp/api.py, store.py, business.py, chat.py, events.py, notify.py) that reuses the Telegram bot's storage/agents/tools layers against a shared Supabase project, plus dev (SQLite/mock) vs prod (Supabase/live) run modes, the API contract, SSE realtime design, and known technical debt (no auth, mock charts, chat persistence gaps).
nodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; documents a dual-mode (dev/prod) architecture with an ASCII system diagram and explicit data-boundary rules between dashboard-owned web_* tables and the bot's production tables.

nodes.name: DECISIONS.md
nodes.summary: ADR-lite decision log for the Web App with 29 numbered decisions (D-001 to D-029) covering architecture (pluggable SQLite/Supabase store, shared Supabase with the bot), product strategy (Max-centric vs form-first entry, packaging tiers, golden-path milestones), and process rules (no fake UI, auth deferred, content calendar model), each tagged with status.
nodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese ADR-lite format (one-line decisions in a table); later entries supersede or reverse earlier ones (e.g., D-026 reverses D-007), reflecting an append-only, never-delete decision history.

nodes.name: PRODUCT.md
nodes.summary: Product requirements draft (PRD) for the Web App defining the value proposition (founders chat with virtual CMO 'Max' to get a marketing strategy), target users segmented by subscription tier (Starter/Pro/Agency), core jobs-to-be-done, the v1 golden-path outcomes (diagnosis-to-strategy, then content production), experience principles, the monthly-subscription business model, and explicit out-of-scope items like auth and Ads optimization.
nodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese PRD with placeholder questions (❓) awaiting founder input; every feature is meant to trace back to a section in this file.

nodes.name: ROADMAP.md
nodes.summary: Milestone roadmap (M0-M4) for the Web App following a 'deepen one golden path before expanding' principle: M0 establishes one fully real end-to-end user flow, M1 completes the diagnosis-to-strategy stage, M2 ships real content production, M3 adds live Ads/competitor operations, and M4 delivers auth, billing, and observability for public readiness.
nodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese roadmap using checkbox task lists per milestone and an explicit priority rule set (fix golden path before adding pages).

nodes.name: WAY_OF_WORKING.md
nodes.summary: Working agreement between the Founder and the Engineer/CTO (Claude) defining five core principles (spec-first, vertical-slice depth, no fake UI, docs/web/ as single source of truth, decisions get recorded), the per-slice workflow loop, the Definition of Done checklist, quality/risk gates (LLM cost, security, privacy, FB API degradation), a 1-pager template, technical conventions, and triggers requiring the CTO to stop and ask the Founder.
nodes.languageNotes: Vietnamese process contract; codifies a spec-then-code, single-vertical-slice methodology with a reusable 1-pager template and explicit escalation triggers.

nodes.name: M0-directional-reframe.md
nodes.summary: Spec (D-029) reframing the M0 web synthesis output as strategic 'direction' rather than committed SMART numbers, covering intake field alignment, output presentation changes, and removal of dead Max-chat UI, with acceptance criteria and a Vietnamese-language plan to fix three identified inconsistencies between the web wizard and the bot's discovery/synthesis logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; references specific code symbols (web/app.js INTAKE_STEPS, agents/discovery.REQUIRED_FIELDS) as evidence for the spec's acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: M0-discovery-to-strategy.md
nodes.summary: One-pager planning slice for milestone M0 describing the end-to-end golden path from chat-based business discovery through diagnostic pipeline execution to rendering a real 90-day strategy page, including scope, risks, dependencies, and founder-approved decisions.
nodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; lists explicit module dependencies (agents/discovery.py, agents/pipeline.py, agents/strategy.py, storage/v2/strategies.py, webapp/business.py, webapp/chat.py) as the slice's 'Phụ thuộc' section.

nodes.name: M1-content-production.md

nodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; references domain logic source modules (agents/operational_prompts.py, agents/campaign_ideation.py, agents/campaign_scope_library.py) that the web layer must mirror.

nodes.name: output-reader-editor.md

nodes.languageNotes: Written in Vietnamese; ties feature F3 directly to the backend function agents/surgical_edit.patch_document and new API routes in webapp/api.py.

nodes.name: ads_notifier.py
nodes.summary: Builds and sends Facebook Ads performance digests and alerts: pulls/snapshots ad account metrics, computes deltas, formats daily/weekly/quick reports, detects threshold alerts, and safely sends Telegram messages.

nodes.name: pull_and_snapshot
nodes.summary: Decrypts a connection's access token, fetches current Facebook ad account insights, and saves a daily snapshot while updating the last-pull timestamp.

nodes.name: backfill_snapshots
nodes.summary: Backfills historical daily ad insight snapshots for a connection across a configurable number of past days, grouping results by date and persisting each.

nodes.name: compute_delta
nodes.summary: Computes aggregate and average metric deltas between today's and previous period's campaign rows for use in digest reports.

nodes.name: format_daily_digest
nodes.summary: Formats a human-readable daily performance digest message including account health, deltas, alerts, and top-performing campaign.

nodes.name: format_weekly_digest
nodes.summary: Formats a weekly performance digest comparing this week's and previous week's aggregated ad metrics.

nodes.name: fetch_quick_report
nodes.summary: Fetches and assembles ad performance data for a quick on-demand report over a specified comparison period.

nodes.name: format_quick_report
nodes.summary: Formats the on-demand quick report message comparing current period metrics against the prior comparison period.

nodes.name: ads_table_from_metrics
nodes.summary: Builds a formatted table of individual ad performance rows from tracked metrics for inclusion in reports.

nodes.name: _find_alerts
nodes.summary: Scans campaigns for performance conditions (e.g. high CPA, low CTR) and produces a list of alert candidates.

nodes.name: check_alerts
nodes.summary: Evaluates campaigns against current and previous metrics to determine which alerts should fire, respecting per-alert cooldown windows.

nodes.name: format_alert
nodes.summary: Formats a single triggered alert into a human-readable Telegram message.

nodes.name: _find_winner
nodes.summary: Identifies the best-performing campaign among a set based on a chosen metric, used to highlight a winner in digests.

nodes.name: send_message_safe
nodes.summary: Sends a Telegram message with markdown formatting, falling back to plain text and logging warnings if the markdown send fails.

nodes.name: ads_scheduler.py
nodes.summary: Background scheduler that periodically triggers daily/weekly ad digests, alert monitoring, token refresh, snapshot backfill, and cleanup jobs based on time-of-day checks.

nodes.name: start_ads_scheduler
nodes.summary: Starts the background ads scheduler loop that periodically ticks to trigger scheduled digest, alert, refresh, and cleanup jobs.

nodes.name: _tick
nodes.summary: Checks the current time against scheduled fire windows and dispatches async tasks for weekly report, daily digest, alert monitor, token refresh, and cleanup jobs.

nodes.name: _run_daily_digest
nodes.summary: Runs the daily digest job for all active ad connections, pulling fresh snapshots, computing deltas, and sending formatted digest messages.

nodes.name: _run_weekly_report
nodes.summary: Runs the weekly report job for all active connections, fetching snapshot ranges and sending formatted weekly digests.

nodes.name: _run_alert_monitor
nodes.summary: Runs the alert monitoring job that pulls fresh snapshots, checks alert conditions, and sends triggered alerts to users.

nodes.name: _run_token_refresh
nodes.summary: Refreshes Facebook access tokens for all active connections nearing expiry, handling revoked tokens via the revocation handler.

nodes.name: _run_snapshot_backfill
nodes.summary: Runs a one-time backfill of historical snapshots for all active connections at scheduler startup.

nodes.name: _run_cleanup
nodes.summary: Triggers cleanup of old ad snapshot records beyond the retention window.

nodes.name: _handle_token_revoked
nodes.summary: Disables a connection whose Facebook token was revoked and notifies the user via Telegram.

nodes.name: fb_connections.py
nodes.summary: Data access layer for Facebook Ads account connections, OAuth state, ad snapshots, and alert cooldowns, backed by Supabase tables (user_fb_connections, oauth_states, ads_snapshots, ads_alert_cooldowns).

nodes.name: save_connection
nodes.summary: Upserts a Facebook Ads connection record for a user, storing the encrypted token, active account, expiry, and available accounts list.

nodes.name: update_active_account
nodes.summary: Updates the active ad account id and name for a user's stored Facebook connection.

nodes.name: get_available_accounts
nodes.summary: Retrieves and parses the list of available Facebook ad accounts stored for a user's connection.

nodes.name: get_connection
nodes.summary: Fetches a user's stored Facebook Ads connection record from Supabase.

nodes.name: get_all_active_connections
nodes.summary: Retrieves all Facebook connections with notifications enabled, used by the scheduler to drive digest and alert jobs.

nodes.name: update_last_pull
nodes.summary: Updates the last-pull timestamp for a user's Facebook connection record.

nodes.name: update_token
nodes.summary: Updates the encrypted access token and expiry timestamp for a user's connection.

nodes.name: update_notification_settings
nodes.summary: Updates arbitrary notification preference fields for a user's Facebook connection via keyword arguments.

nodes.name: delete_connection
nodes.summary: Deletes a user's Facebook Ads connection record from Supabase.

nodes.name: disable_connection
nodes.summary: Disables notifications for a user's Facebook connection, typically called when a token is revoked.

nodes.name: save_oauth_state
nodes.summary: Persists a one-time OAuth state token tied to a user id with an expiry timestamp for CSRF protection during the OAuth flow.

nodes.name: consume_oauth_state
nodes.summary: Validates and consumes (deletes) an OAuth state token, checking expiry before returning the associated user id.

nodes.name: compute_campaign_metrics
nodes.summary: Computes derived campaign performance metrics (CTR, CPA, ROAS, etc.) from raw Facebook insight action data.

nodes.name: save_snapshot
nodes.summary: Persists a daily snapshot of computed campaign metrics for a user to the ads_snapshots table.

nodes.name: _extract_action
nodes.summary: Extracts a numeric action count of a given type from a campaign's raw Facebook insights actions array.

nodes.name: _extract_action_value
nodes.summary: Extracts a monetary action value of a given type from a campaign's raw Facebook insights action_values array.

nodes.name: get_snapshot
nodes.summary: Fetches the saved campaign snapshot for a user on a specific date.

nodes.name: get_snapshots_range
nodes.summary: Fetches saved campaign snapshots for a user within a date range, used for weekly comparison reports.

nodes.name: cleanup_old_snapshots
nodes.summary: Deletes ad snapshot records older than the 90-day retention window.

nodes.name: check_and_set_cooldown

layers.name: Telegram Bot Interface

layers.name: Multi-Agent Strategy Pipeline
layers.description: The core marketing-intelligence engine: the orchestrator and per-stage agents (discovery, market/competitor research, customer insight, pricing psychology, social listening, campaign ideation/intake/execution, funnel mapping, strategy generation, content critique) plus the SAVE/SMART strategic frameworks and KPI/pricing-segment libraries they draw on.

layers.name: Web Dashboard & Chat API
layers.description: The FastAPI/Starlette backend powering the browser dashboard and Max chat assistant: REST endpoints, event streaming, Supabase/SQLite-backed stores, business/account views, notifications, and the standalone-build packager.

layers.name: Static Web Frontend
layers.description: The static HTML/CSS/JS dashboard client (standalone and hosted variants) that renders campaign, content, and ads data served by the webapp API.

layers.name: Storage & Data Model
layers.description: Persistence layer covering domain models (Session, BusinessProfile, BrandVoice), the v2 Supabase-backed repositories (briefs, campaigns, engagements, posts, profiles, strategies, users), Facebook connection/oauth/ads-snapshot storage, and the full SQL migration history defining the relational schema.

layers.name: Integrations, Background Jobs & Tool Belt
layers.description: Cross-cutting capabilities used by agents and the bot: the Facebook OAuth/Ads service and its notifier/scheduler background workers, competitor-monitoring and feedback-digest jobs, the LLM router, crypto/token-billing utilities, and image/video/transcription generation clients.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: End-to-end test suites validating the orchestrator pipeline, content-generation pipeline, web API, and persona-menu behavior.

layers.name: Configuration, Deployment & Product Docs
layers.description: Root-level runtime entry points and environment/config files (config.py, run_local.py, run_web.py, .env.example, railway.json, Procfile), the GitHub Pages deploy workflow, and the product/architecture documentation, backlog, and task notes describing how the system is built and shipped.

layers.name: AI Dev-Assistant Tooling
layers.description: Claude Code plugin assets bundled in the repo (.claude/) — custom agents, slash commands, and skill packs used to assist development of this project, not part of the shipped product runtime.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what Marketing OS is: an AI-powered marketing strategy platform built for Vietnamese founders and small business owners, delivered as a Telegram bot. It outlines the product's core features, installation steps, and directory structure, giving you the map you'll use for the rest of this tour.

tour.title: The Bot's Entry Point
tour.description: bot/main.py is where the application actually starts. It builds the python-telegram-bot Application object, registers every command, message, and callback handler, and wires up the webhook that receives Telegram updates. This is the highest-scoring entry point in the codebase and the natural place to begin tracing how a user's message flows through the system.

tour.title: Handling Conversations and Configuration
tour.description: Building on main.py, bot/handlers.py is the busiest module reached at this depth (35 outgoing dependencies) — it implements every slash command, free-text conversation branch, and callback dispatch that drives the chat experience. Alongside it, config.py is one of the two most depended-upon files in the entire project (19 incoming references), centralizing environment variables, API keys, and feature flags that nearly every other module reads from.

tour.title: Storage and the Web API Surface
tour.description: Two more direct dependencies of main.py round out the system's foundation. storage/__init__.py re-exports the persistence layer (sessions, brand voice, campaign history, embeddings) used throughout the bot, while webapp/api.py defines the full Starlette route table for the browser dashboard — with 79 outgoing edges, it is the single highest fan-out file in the project, touching nearly every other subsystem to serve the web dashboard and chat assistant.

tour.title: The Multi-Agent Strategy Pipeline
tour.description: agents/pipeline.py is the orchestration core of the product: it sequences the marketing-intelligence pipeline through intake, market research, competitor analysis, pricing psychology, customer insight, and more. This is where the 'multi-agent' part of Marketing OS comes alive — each stage is a discrete agent contributing to one cohesive strategy output for the founder using the bot.

tour.title: Prompts, Tasks, and Output Shaping
tour.description: The pipeline relies on a cluster of supporting modules: agents/prompts.py holds the static prompt templates for every pipeline stage, agents/task_registry.py defines the catalog of marketing tasks and the intake fields each one requires, and agents/output_formats.py supplies formatting and localization instructions that steer how LLM responses are structured (bullets, tables, language). Together these three modules show how the agents are instructed and how their outputs are kept consistent.

tour.title: Strategic Frameworks and the Bot's UI
tour.description: Two domain-specific frameworks feed the pipeline's strategy stage: save_framework.py generates a structured Solution/Access/Value/Education breakdown, and smart_framework.py produces SMART goal templates scaled to a business's stage. On the user-facing side, bot/keyboards.py builds the inline keyboard menus that let founders navigate the conversation generated by this pipeline.

tour.title: Quality Control and Agent Skills
tour.description: Before a strategy reaches the user, agents/critic.py runs an LLM-based quality pass over the pipeline's output, catching meta-review artifacts and injecting source links. agents/skills.py defines the AgentSkill base class and the concrete skill implementations (market research, competitor analysis, pricing psychology, social listening) — its AgentSkill class has the highest fan-in of any class in the codebase, meaning nearly every strategic capability is built on this shared abstraction.

tour.title: The Data Model
tour.description: storage/models.py is the single most depended-upon file in the entire project (20 incoming references), defining the domain models — Session, BusinessProfile, BrandVoice — that every other layer ultimately reads or writes. The migration history in storage/migrations/006_normalize_schema.sql shows how that model maps to the relational schema: a large normalization migration restructuring the database into six core tables including users, campaigns, and skill_runs, which is the persistent backbone behind everything you've seen the bot and pipeline do so far.

tour.title: Cross-Cutting Tools: the LLM Router
tour.description: tools/llm_router.py is a high fan-out utility (34 outgoing edges) used across agents and the bot to route requests to different LLM providers. It centralizes model selection and API calls so that pipeline stages like the ones in Step 6 don't need to know which underlying provider is actually generating their content — a key abstraction for keeping the multi-agent system provider-agnostic.

tour.title: The Web Dashboard Frontend
tour.description: While the Telegram bot is the primary interface, web/app.js powers a static web dashboard that visualizes campaign, content, and ads data served by webapp/api.py from Step 4. webapp/store_supabase.py backs that API with a Supabase-based store (51 outgoing edges), translating REST/SSE requests into queries against the schema you saw in Step 9.

tour.title: Runtime Configuration and Deployment
tour.description: The .env.example file documents every environment variable the application needs at runtime — Telegram tokens, API keys, and feature flags consumed by config.py from Step 3. Procfile and railway.json define how the app actually gets run in production: Procfile declares the web process that launches the bot's entry point, while railway.json overrides that process configuration specifically for deployment on the Railway platform.

tour.title: Continuous Deployment of the Static Dashboard
tour.description: The GitHub Actions workflow in .github/workflows/deploy-pages.yml automates publishing the static web/ dashboard from Step 11 to GitHub Pages. This is the capstone of the infrastructure story: every change to the frontend is automatically built and deployed without manual intervention, closing the loop from code change to live dashboard.
